content.problemStatement:
**This problem has subtasks. You will receive 303030 points for solving the K≤NK \leq NK≤N case.**

You're given an array AAA containing NNN integers.

You must perform the following operation **exactly** KKK times:


Choose an index iii (1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N), and multiply AiA_iAi​ by 222.

That is, replace AiA_iAi​ with 2⋅Ai2\cdot A_i2⋅Ai​.
Find the minimum possible sum of the array AAA after all KKK operations have been performed.

This sum can be quite large, so print it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — the length of the array and the number of operations to be performed.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — the initial elements of the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum possible final sum, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: It's optimal to use both moves to multiply A1A_1A1​ by 222, resulting in the array [4,3,9,27][4, 3, 9, 27][4,3,9,27]. The sum of this array is 434343.
Test case 222: We have no moves, so just output the sum of the array.
Test case 333: It's optimal to use one move each on both A1A_1A1​ and A2A_2A2​.
The resulting array is [2000000000,1000000008][2000000000, 1000000008][2000000000,1000000008], with a sum of 300000000830000000083000000008.
Modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7, this is 999999994999999994999999994.

The next test can appear only in subtask 222.
